message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Boceprevir is an HCV NSP3 inhibitor that has been explored as a repurposed drug for COVID-19. It inhibits the SARS-CoV-2 main protease (M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  ) and contains an α-ketoamide warhead, a P1 β-cyclobutylalanyl moiety, a P2 dimethylcyclopropylproline, a P3
                  <jats:italic>tert</jats:italic>
                  -butyl-glycine, and a P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal
                  <jats:italic>tert</jats:italic>
                  -butylcarbamide. By introducing modifications at all four positions, we synthesized 20 boceprevir-based M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  inhibitors including PF-07321332 and characterized their M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  inhibition potency in test tubes (
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  ) and human host cells (
                  <jats:italic>in cellulo</jats:italic>
                  ). Crystal structures of M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  bound with 10 inhibitors and antiviral potency of 4 inhibitors were characterized as well. Replacing the P1 site with a β-(S-2-oxopyrrolidin-3-yl)-alanyl (opal) residue and the warhead with an aldehyde leads to high
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  potency. The original moieties at P2, P3 and the P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal cap positions in boceprevir are better than other tested chemical moieties for high
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  potency. In crystal structures, all inhibitors form a covalent adduct with the M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  active site cysteine. The P1 opal residue, P2 dimethylcyclopropylproline and P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal
                  <jats:italic>tert</jats:italic>
                  -butylcarbamide make strong hydrophobic interactions with M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  , explaining high
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  potency of inhibitors that contain these moieties. A unique observation was made with an inhibitor that contains an P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal isovaleramide. In its M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  complex structure, the P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal isovaleramide is tucked deep in a small pocket of M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  that originally recognizes a P4 alanine side chain in a substrate. Although all inhibitors show high
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  potency, they have drastically different
                  <jats:italic>in cellulo</jats:italic>
                  potency in inhibiting ectopically expressed M
                  <jats:sup>Pro</jats:sup>
                  in human 293T cells. All inhibitors including PF-07321332 with a P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal carbamide or amide have low
                  <jats:italic>in cellulo</jats:italic>
                  potency. This trend is reversed when the P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal cap is changed to a carbamate. The installation of a P3
                  <jats:italic>O-tert</jats:italic>
                  -butyl-threonine improves
                  <jats:italic>in cellulo</jats:italic>
                  potency. Three molecules that contain a P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal carbamate were advanced to antiviral tests on three SARS-CoV-2 variants. They all have high potency with EC
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  values around 1 μM. A control compound with a nitrile warhead and a P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal amide has undetectable antiviral potency. Based on all observations, we conclude that a P4
                  <jats:italic>N</jats:italic>
                  -terminal carbamate in a boceprevir derivative is key for high antiviral potency against SARS-CoV-2.
                </jats:p>
title: The N -Terminal Carbamate is Key to High Cellular and Antiviral Potency for Boceprevir-Based SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease Inhibitors
